mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 when FEDFUNDS declines or stays below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment sectors, generating broad-based earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series diverges, or Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term corporate output and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in early-cycle regimes, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase above 0.3% signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered months, or the inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macro aggregates and consumer spending, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of occurrences, or interaction effects vanish when series are analyzed independently.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a housing market correction signal weakening residential investment and broader credit contraction, which reduces economic activity and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during housing corrections, or the signal shows positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances.
